clinical_history: 74 year old man with a history of coronary artery disease and palpitations. What is the cardiac rhythm?
diagnosis:
The rhythm is atrial fibrillation with a moderate to rapid ventricular response (average ventricular rate around 110 bpm). The ECG also shows nonspecific ST-T changes.

For an overview of the contemporary management of atrial fibrillation, the most common major cardiac arrhythmia in adults, see 2006 guidelines (and any subsequent updates) from the American College of Cardiology/American Heart Association/European Cardiology Society
http://www.acc.org/qualityandscience/clinical/guidelines/atrial_fib/pdfs/AF_Full_Text.pdf
